angle.headline: Why Your M&A Diligence Process Finds Everything Except What Kills Deals
angle.thesis: Middle-market owners spend six figures on diligence that audits the past but never diagnoses whether the target can operate without its founder — the single variable that determines post-close performance.
angle.targetPersona: Business owners in growth-through-acquisition mode, particularly those who've completed one deal and are now considering their second, operating with $10M-$75M revenue across the US-LATAM corridor
angle.relevanceToAudience: With USMCA at its six-year review and nearshoring momentum creating acquisition opportunities across the corridor, owners are evaluating targets in Mexico and LATAM — but standard diligence frameworks were built for US-only deals and miss the founder-dependency risk that is magnified across borders and jurisdictions.

peopleAngle.challenge: The seller's leadership team has never operated without the founder's real-time input, and the buyer's integration team lacks the trust or context to make decisions during the 90-day transition window when founder dependency becomes operationally visible.
peopleAngle.framework: Kotter Step 2: Build the Guiding Coalition

justification.angleChoice: Most M&A diligence answers 'Does this business make money?' but never asks 'Can this business make decisions without the seller in the room?' — the real post-close question.
justification.osPillarChoice: The failure happens because diligence is structured to audit the past (financials, compliance, IP) rather than diagnose the operating model's ability to execute independently — particularly across time zones and legal systems in corridor deals.
justification.peopleAngleChoice: The Machine Test: before you sign the LOI, spend one week asking only this: 'Who makes what decisions when the owner is unavailable?' If the answer is 'it waits' or 'it escalates,' you are buying a job, not a business.

entries.fieldReportSummary: [Meta announced this week](https://www.businessinsider.com/meta-layoffs-internal-memo-ai-job-cuts-faq-2026-5) that it will cut 10% of staff on Wednesday, eliminating around 8,000 roles in three waves of notifications timed to 4am local time across regions. angle.headline: The Board You Built Is Now the Ceiling You Hit
angle.thesis: Most mid-market owners believe their board exists to validate decisions, but the moment validation becomes the only function, the board stops being a governance body and becomes a bottleneck disguised as oversight.
angle.targetPersona: Family business owners ($20M-$100M revenue) preparing for succession or institutional investment, particularly those who built advisory boards of friends, investors, or retired executives who never challenge the founder's instincts
angle.relevanceToAudience: US-LATAM corridor operators face a specific governance trap: boards composed of regional advisors who understand relationships but not operating models, or US investors who understand structure but not the corridor's realities. As private equity interest grows in the region and succession pressures mount, the board that got you here becomes the constraint preventing institutional transition.
talkingPoints: You assembled a board to formalize decision-making, but if every meeting ends with unanimous approval of what you already decided, you built a compliance theater, not a governance body.

The dysfunction appears when you pursue institutional investment, succession planning, or geographic expansion — moments that require the board to challenge your assumptions, not rubber-stamp your instincts.
Jim Collins' Level 5 Leadership applied to boards: great boards require collective humility and disciplined confrontation, not individual brilliance and polite consensus.
Audit your last six board meetings — if no major decision was reversed or delayed due to board challenge, you have a validation body, not a governance body, and that is now your constraint.

peopleAngle.challenge: The founder must shift from treating the board as validators of instinct to treating them as counterweights to certainty, which requires surrendering the psychological safety of being right in every room.

entries.fieldReportSummary: In a 48-hour window this month, a single executive's conviction removed a tool from every operator who had built around it.
